title: When generating host volumes for k8s, force to lowercase
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16542

Kubernetes only allows lower case persistent volume names.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Persistent volume names will be lowercase with podman kube generate
```

comments.body: Does this break the generate kube -> play kube workflow in that now we'll get different volumes than we did before for the container from play? And do we care about that?

> Does this break the generate kube -> play kube workflow in that now we'll get different volumes than we did before for the container from play? And do we care about that?

IIUC this doesn't change the name of the actual volumes. It changes the names in the K8S YAML file. That is how a `volumeMount` entry in the `container` points to a `volume` entry and not the name of the PVC/Volume itself
Correct this does not change paths on disk, without this change the k8s YAML file is broken and could never be run, since upper case names are not supported.
